title: Counterfactual undercut-value features instead of raw degradation proxies
summary: Official F1/Pirelli strategy material emphasizes that pit timing depends on whether fresh-tyre gain can offset pit-loss and rival reactions, while recent game-theoretic work frames pit timing as a competitive sequential decision.
rationale: Official F1/Pirelli strategy material emphasizes that pit timing depends on whether fresh-tyre gain can offset pit-loss and rival reactions, while recent game-theoretic work frames pit timing as a competitive sequential decision. That suggests modeling pit value as a counterfactual delta, not just an absolute tyre-age threshold.
implementation_hint: For each lap, estimate local old-tyre loss from recent pace trend and field-relative lap baselines, estimate fresh-tyre pace for each compound, subtract an approximate circuit pit-loss prior, and add nearest-rival exposure features such as 'car ahead vulnerable to undercut' and 'stop now rejoins into traffic pocket'. Use only contemporaneous or lagged race-lap aggregates.
expected_effect: Should help especially in mid-race cases where raw wear looks moderate but the strategic undercut opportunity is strong.
risk: Pit-loss and fresh-tyre gain are noisy without external timing detail; badly estimated counterfactuals can just duplicate existing features with more noise.
